join_order.output: qualifying_orders (~57 rows: o_orderkey, o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice)

join_order.strategy: index_point_lookup per qualifying order on o_custkey
join_order.output: joined_result (~57 rows with c_name, c_custkey added)

join_order.strategy: index_point_lookup per qualifying o_orderkey, sum l_quantity over matched positions (~7 rows avg per orderkey)

filter_pushdown.note: ~57 qualifying orderkeys out of 15M groups; filter applied after full hash aggregation

late_materialization.trigger: load c_name only for ~57 qualifying customer row indices after all joins complete

data_structures.qualifying_orderkey_set: compact_flat_hash_set(capacity=128, key=int32_t) — ~57 entries, fits entirely in L1 cache
data_structures.result_tuples: flat_struct_array(capacity=128) — final assembled tuples for TopK

parallelism.strategy: morsel_driven for subquery scan; sequential for index-lookup phases
notes: Phase 1 (subquery): 64 threads each scan ~937K lineitem rows; thread i owns hash partitions where (key_hash % 64 == i) using partition-local hash maps; no cross-thread contention
Phase 2-5: Single-threaded index lookups — only ~57 qualifying entries, parallelism overhead exceeds benefit

index_usage.purpose: point_lookup per qualifying l_orderkey (~57 probes) to get orders row_idx; avoids full 15M-row orders scan
point_lookup per qualifying o_custkey (~57 probes) to get customer row_idx for c_name and c_custkey; avoids full 1.5M-row customer scan
lookup all lineitem row positions per qualifying o_orderkey (~57 lookups, ~7 rows avg each) to compute SUM(l_quantity) for outer query; avoids second full lineitem scan
index_usage.layout: cap=33554432, n_pos=59986052; entries: [int32_t key, uint32_t offset, uint32_t count]; positions: uint32_t[59986052]

physical_plan.scan_strategy: single_pass_fused for subquery (l_orderkey + l_quantity, 60M rows, 720MB sequential); index_driven for all subsequent phases
physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential for Phase 1 lineitem scan; random_access_with_prefetch for index lookups in Phases 2-4

phase_details.name: subquery_lineitem_full_scan_and_hash_agg
phase_details.description: Parallel morsel-driven scan of l_orderkey.bin + l_quantity.bin (60M rows). Each of 64 threads processes morsels of 65536 rows. Partitioned aggregation: key routes to partition (key_hash & 63), each thread accumulates into its own 64-partition local hash maps. After scan, merge partitions across threads (thread i merges all partition-i maps). Collect orderkeys where sum_qty > 300.0 into compact hash set.

phase_details.name: orders_index_lookup
phase_details.description: For each of ~57 qualifying orderkeys, probe orders_orderkey_hash to get row_idx. Load o_custkey[row_idx], o_orderdate[row_idx], o_totalprice[row_idx]. Store as flat array of order structs.

phase_details.name: customer_index_lookup
phase_details.description: For each qualifying order, probe customer_custkey_hash on o_custkey → row_idx. Load c_custkey[row_idx]. Defer c_name load (late materialization — load from offsets_data only for final 57 qualifying rows).

phase_details.name: lineitem_qty_index_agg
phase_details.description: For each qualifying o_orderkey, probe lineitem_orderkey_hash → {offset, count}. Iterate positions[offset..offset+count), sum l_quantity[pos] for each. Result: sum_qty per orderkey.

phase_details.name: topk_and_output
phase_details.description: Assemble 57 result tuples: (c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum_qty). std::sort with comparator (o_totalprice DESC, o_orderdate ASC). Emit first 100 rows (all 57 qualify since result < 100). Format o_orderdate as YYYY-MM-DD from epoch days. Decode c_name from offsets_data during final emit.
phase_details.note: With only 57 results, all rows are output (< LIMIT 100). No heap needed — simple sort.

estimated_cost.bottleneck: 720MB sequential I/O for 60M lineitem rows in subquery phase; parallelized across 64 threads with partitioned hash aggregation to avoid contention
estimated_cost.index_benefit: Indexes eliminate 2 full table scans (15M orders + 1.5M customer + second 60M lineitem scan), saving ~1.9B row reads
